value_chain_position: Jet engine OEM and aftermarket service provider (LEAP, GE9X, F414/F110 military); sells engines as loss leaders and monetizes long-term service agreements — a classic installed-base toll model.
relative_comps: GE Aerospace and Safran (via the CFM joint venture) together power ~70% of single-aisle commercial aviation globally — an entrenched duopoly. Military LEAP derivatives and the F414 make GE a critical DoD supplier. Revenue growth (22% YoY) is driven by aftermarket pricing power as the post-COVID fleet ramp fills MRO slots. At $345B market cap, multiples already reflect the toll-taker status; the pure disruption premium vs a mature prime is modest. RTX Pratt & Whitney is the only peer at scale on both commercial and military.
